sections.title: Driving while disqualified or suspended
sections.content: Every person commits an offence who drives a motor vehicle on a road while disqualified from driving or while their licence is suspended. Maximum penalty varies based on circumstances.

sections.title: Driving contrary to limited licence
sections.content: Every person commits an offence who drives in contravention of the conditions of a limited licence (work licence).

sections.title: Driving while forbidden
sections.content: Every person commits an offence who drives while forbidden to drive by a Police officer. This includes being stopped by Police and instructed not to drive.

sections.title: Driving in contravention of alcohol interlock licence
sections.content: Every person commits an offence who drives in contravention of the conditions of an alcohol interlock licence, including driving a vehicle without an approved interlock device.

sections.title: Reckless or dangerous driving
sections.content: Every person commits an offence who operates a vehicle recklessly or dangerously. Dangerous driving involves driving that creates a significant risk to public safety.

sections.title: Careless or inconsiderate driving
sections.content: Every person commits an offence who operates a vehicle carelessly or without reasonable consideration for other persons using the road.

sections.title: Duty to stop after accident
sections.content: The driver of a vehicle involved in an accident must stop and ascertain whether any person has been injured, and must give their name and address and vehicle details.

sections.title: Power of Police to require persons to supply information
sections.content: Any constable may require any driver of a vehicle to state their full name, full address, date of birth, and occupation, and to produce their driver licence for inspection.

sections.title: Compulsory blood test
sections.content: Police may require a blood test if a constable has good cause to suspect a person has committed an offence against section 65 (duty to stop after accident) involving injury or death.

sections.title: Person incapable of consenting to blood test
sections.content: Where a person is unconscious or otherwise incapable of consenting, a medical practitioner or medical officer may take a blood sample if the constable has good cause to suspect an offence.
